videos.title: Best Things To Do in Taipei Taiwan 4K
videos.description:
TAIPEI, TAIWAN: Are you looking for the best things to do in Taipei, Taiwan? We just created a list of 21 must do activities for you to do while visiting Taipei! In this Taipei travel guide we show you around Taipei 101, Night Markets, Temples, National Palace Museum and even take a ride on the MRT. We also show you many Taipei attractions like the National Palace Museum, the old town streets, as well as the lanterns in Shifen, Jiufen, the waterfalls  and much more! 

Best Things To Do in Taipei Taiwan 2025
(TIMESTAMPS)
0:00 Taipei Intro
0:44 Taipei 101
1:10 Chiang Kai-Shek Memorial
1:34 Elephant Mountain
1:51 National Taiwan Museum
2:19 Longshan Temple
3:15 Shifen Lanterns
3:43 Golden Waterfall
4:05 Jiufin
4:31 Local Food
5:46 Raohe St. Night Market
6:13 MRT
6:38 Taiwan High Speed Rail
6:57 Dihua Street
7:30 Ximen
8:09 Red House
8:20 Miramar Ferris Wheel
8:35 National Palace Museum
9:26 Performing Arts Center
9:48 Shilin Market
10:34 Meow Planet
11:19 Nanya Night Market

Island Hopper TV takes you around the world to the premiere travel destinations. Our virtual travel guides from across America to Asia, Europe and Australia have assisted many world travelers in educating themselves on their next destination to travel or even vacation.
videos.transcript: [Music] thank you what's going on everybody this is Island Hopper TV and today we're going to talk about the best things to do while visiting Taipei Taiwan let's do it that's right everybody we're going to show you the top things to do while visiting here in Taipei there will be time stamps below so you can bounce around from location to location let's go ahead and get into it and first up we're headed to Taipei 101 at the time of its completion in 2004 it was the world's tallest building standing 508 meters that's 1 667 feet that includes the Spire at the top it did Hold This Record until 2010 when the Burj Khalifa in Dubai was completed and the basic ticket to go to the 89th floor is 600 Taiwanese dollars for adults next up we're headed over to the shanghai-shek Memorial Hall this is actually considered one of the iconic landmarks here he was the former president of the Republic of China which is now Taiwan at this location you'll find the national Concert Hall as well as the National Theater the Memorial Hall is located inside at Liberty Square which is a large open area that is considered a hub for festivals and large gatherings okay now here we are at Elephant Mountain this is actually a hike to the top of this peak right here in the city the reason people typically hike Elephant Mountain is to get the panoramic views overlooking Taipei Skyline including a good look at Taipei 101. typically a 20 to 30 minute hike the national Taiwan museum is actually the oldest Museum here in the Republic of China also known as Taiwan dating all the way back to when Japan ruled over Taiwan for over 40 years that happened actually this Museum here is not to be confused with the National Palace Museum it's a bit smaller and it's located in Zhong Zhang District the history of Taiwan is quite fascinating so the more you know the more educated you can become about what's going on in the world today now here we are at the longshin temple which is actually one of the oldest temples in Taipei originally established in 1738 by settlers from fujian China the temple is dedicated to a variety of deities including Guan Yin who is the goddess of Mercy as well as Buddha some interesting history I'll tell you real quick the actual Nation as we know it Taiwan was originally founded by The ROC in 1912. Taiwan was under Taiwan was a colony of the Netherlands for about 40 years in the mid 17th century fast forward to 1895 it became a colony of Japan under the Treaty of shimanoseki when the Qing Dynasty handed over Taiwan to Japan but in 1945 after World War II Japan surrendered Taiwan to the actual Roc the Republic of China not to be confused with the CC P which is called the Chinese Communist party which is located in Beijing now we're going to head about 30 minutes north of Taipei to the shift in Old Town where you release these Kong Ming lanters also known as Chinese lanterns into the sky made from paper and bamboo they're filled with hot air from an open flame underneath and on the outside you write affirmations that you would like to send up into the heavens each color of paper that you write your affirmations has a different significance the area north of Taipei is very interesting they have many different waterfalls like this one Falls Cascade right into the ocean here and the reason the water is that color is because this is actually very mineral rich soil next up is jufen this is a must-see I highly recommend going to this Village especially in evening time it is known for its fog and great views overlooking the ocean towards the north but it was an old gold mining town and a Japanese colony during a prosperous era of the 20th century when you come up here make sure you walk the old Street of jufen at night next up let's talk about local food sausage okay this is a Taiwanese meatball here it's a red meatball actually all right so we got some duck head some duck tongue and gizzards some duck neck feet well I am trying the chicken feed still I'm not so sure if I like it or not I don't know well they got some barbecue steak here this is going to be torched let's try it on the barbecue [Music] what a show and that one says absolutely delicious that is kimchi Taiwanese and we got some fried chicken here a little bit of like Mayo sauce it looks like some chili broccoli this is actually called pork bone soup this is Tom Yum noodle with pork interesting it's like a lock song all right I got a seafood soup here with noodle shrimp shellfish foreign places to go and try street food is going to be rauhi Street Market this is a night market that takes place basically every night assuming weather allows because they do get plenty of typhoon activity out here in Taipei and all across Taiwan really food isn't the only thing they serve here but it's the main attraction that's made this place I believe the best night market in Taipei there are a few that we'll show you actually together stands for Mass Rapid Transit now Metro Taipei is considered one of the best metros in the world operated and run by the government owned company Taipei Rapid Transit Corporation there's five different lines and over 117 stations averaging over 2.1 million passengers per day that's incredible next up I recommend riding the Taiwan High-Speed Rail it goes over 350 kilometers per hour that's very fast the travel time from Taipei in the north to kaohsong in the south is 90 minutes if you do want to ride the HSR here in Taiwan just go on to Google and search for the nearby station and now here we are at diwas Street this is a popular Street to walk at night but we're showing up here a little bit earlier than that it's around three o'clock in the afternoon the old Street here is definitely of historical significance located in the heart of Taipei the street is preserved traditional architecture as it was a major trading hub for the Qing Dynasty including Chinese medicines herbs textiles and traditional foods popular with locals and tourists alike there's also an ancient Temple here called the city God temple from here we're headed to Jaime this is a trendy shopping and Entertainment District known for its youthful atmosphere the roads often called xyman Ding which is the full name while here consider trying Taiwanese fried chicken with brown sugar Boba milk I would compare this to Shibuya in Tokyo or even go so far as to call it the Times Square of Taiwan if you're really looking for the modern essence of Taipei this is where you need to go and if you're low loving this video so far I would recommend you check the description below because we made a Tokyo things to do video as well as Hong Kong which I encourage you to watch both of those next also while you're in xyman ding I do recommend checking out the Red House theater this is also called Low red Playhouse there is free admission and people like to come here and just take a picture in front of the structure and if you're looking to get high in the sky you got to check out the Miramar Ferris wheel this is out here at the Miramar Mall a popular place in Northern Taipei where they also have an IMAX theater now here we are at the National Palace Museum this is one of taiwan's most iconic cultural institutions with over 700 000 items ranging from paintings to calligraphy Ceramics and other historical relics from mainland China like these Imperial Glass snuff bottles they're impressive especially the ones that are made out of jade so this National Palace Museum has a lot of Art and history here like snuff I didn't really know much about snuff but now I do aside from learning about snuff you also learn about Jade in the importance of Jade in Chinese culture known as the stone of Heaven it's also considered a lucky stone that's why they are always wearing Jade bracelets and necklace the saying is gold is valuable while Jade is priceless now here we are at the Taipei Performing Arts Center it's known for its unique spherical design outside of the actual Theater which holds around 800 seats in the Grand Theater and is an asymmetrical shaped building with 1500 seats just down the street from the Taipei Performing Arts Center is the actual Shilling Market this is another very popular Market where you can get street food and walk around and enjoy the traditional Vibes there's around 30 night markets around the Taipei area and around Taiwan itself there's about 70 of them so these night markets are very popular I'm not going to name them all here but chilling and Rahi is definitely two that you should consider just some interesting facts about Taiwan it's often confused with Thailand so don't get that confused also it's the most mountainous island in the world so lots of mountains out here in Taiwan in fact there are several National Parks worth checking out here if you love nature taroko National Park is one that I would suggest and for those of you who want to try a Taiwanese indoor gaming center tried the owl Planet this isn't your average gaming center this is actually where you go to try and get groceries for an affordable price using one of these claws it sure is a fun way to get groceries but I'm not convinced you save money see people are grabbing the shopping bins to do their Shopping kids love it well if you love noodles you definitely got to come to one of these places because you can get a good deal if you're lucky enough to uh get it for 10 cents let's just say I tried several times to get these Pringles and had zero luck so I don't know how good these claws are but for me personally I'm not any good at it I mean really how do you grab a box of chocolates like that with the claw next up is actually the nanya night market this is actually one of my favorite night markets in all of the Town it is in New Taipei City right next to meow planet now for my final thoughts on Taipei I would recommend you come here for around three to four days to explore getting out in the nature going to the smaller villages in the north where they did the mining and then just hanging out down in the city center for about a day or two doing the night markets hope you guys enjoyed this one if you did please hit the like button and consider subscribing to the channel also watch our Hong Kong and Tokyo videos next here by clicking those videos on the end screens foreign

videos.transcript: Are you planning for Taipei? [Music] Taipei. [Music] Join us on a thrilling adventure as we unpack 24 hours in the heart of Taiwan's vibrant capital. From the lively Schillin night market to the historic corridors of Bopiliao historical block, our day is a tapestry woven with culture, history, and of course, a myriad of delicious eats. Whether your time in Taipei is a brief getaway or an extended exploration, these are the seven essential places you must not miss. Our Taipei escapade kicked off with an evening arrival and we wasted no time immersing ourselves in the city's culinary delights. The first stop, the legendary Schilling Night Market, a street food area bursting with local flavors and energy. [Music] [Music] [Music] [Music] As the sun rose for Taipei. We woke up in the heart of the Jong Jang district where we chose our hotel to be. Our footsteps led us through the serene 228 Peace Park which was on our way to the majestic Chiian Kaishek Memorial Hall, a symbol of Taiwan's history and resilience. We opted to walk, soaking in every bit of Taipei's charm. Our [Music] next destination transported us back in time to The Biliao historical block. A hidden gem, this area preserves the essence of Taipei's past with its narrow lanes and wellpreserved architecture. It's a must visit for history buffs and wanderers alike. [Music] [Music] With history on our minds, it was time to refuel. We dove into a bowl of mouthwatering ramen, a testament to Taipe's diverse culinary scene. A cozy coffee shop provided the perfect backdrop for sipping coffee and savoring some dectable sweets. [Music] Our journey continued with a visit to the Banker Langan Temple, a spiritual haven in the heart of the city. The intricate architecture and vibrant atmosphere left us in awe, offering a moment of tranquility amid the urban bustle. [Music] Time for something sweet. She Men, a lively and popular area, beckoned us with its vibrant energy. We indulged in refreshing mango shaved ice and of course an authentic made bubble tea at Ching Fu Tang. [Music] No visit to Taipei is complete without standing in the shadow of the iconic Taipei 101. [Music] Majestic and or inspiring, this architectural marvel took us to new heights, offering panoramic views of the city below. [Music] As our day in Taipei drew to a close, we chased the sunset to Elephant Mountain. A hike rewarded with breathtaking views of Taipei's skyline as the city lights began to glimmer. A fitting farewell to this extraordinary city. [Music]

videos.title: 4 Days in Taipei, Taiwan 2026 - A Travel Guide for First Time Visitors
videos.description:
#taipei #taiwan #travel 
A lot of you asked about my color grading 🙏
I’ve released my cinematic LUT pack + TwoClick PowerGrade here: https://lais.store/
These are the exact tools I use for my travel documentaries.
____________________________
In this travel documentary, I’ll show you how to spend 4 unforgettable days in Taipei — from exploring nostalgic streets and hidden cafés to hiking mountain viewpoints and experiencing the city’s vibrant night markets.
Whether it’s your first time visiting or you’re returning for more, this guide covers the perfect mix of cultural landmarks, scenic spots, local food, and day trips beyond the city — including Yangmingshan National Park, Jiufen Village, and some of the best skyline views of Taipei 101.
__________________

As promised, here are a few things to know that will make traveling in Taiwan a lot smoother. First, before arriving, you'll need to fill out the Taiwan arrival card, which you can easily complete online before your flight. Second, Google Maps is king and works perfectly for both city navigation and public transport. Third, for getting around, Uber and Bolt both work perfectly and are a reliable way to order taxis throughout the city. Fourth, always carry some cash, especially for night markets and smaller local spots. And last, get yourself an easy card. videos.transcript: Taipei is a diverse City with amazing food and friendly locals this is Katie from creative travel guide and today we share the things to know before you go to Taipei looking for more travel tips make sure to hit subscribe and ring that notification Bell so firstly let's look at the best time to visit the best time to visit Taipei is from March to May when the city begins to warm up from the cold winter but the touristy areas are still fairly quiet you can also see the cherry blossom during these spring months and rainfall is fairly light compared to other months typhoon season runs between July and September and this time is also hot and humid so try to avoid if possible so now let's look at getting there most visitors visiting Taiwan will arrive via plane with Taipei having two international airports with most flights arriving at taoan airport from here you can catch the airport MRT which is the fastest route into the City and takes around 35 minutes if you're arriving late at night you can also catch a bus into the city instead if you are visiting other areas in Taiwan you can also catch a high-speed train to most other cities in the country so now let's look at getting around the easiest way to get around Taipei is by taking the metro which has six lines of underground ground and elevated tracks taking you to most areas of the capital you could also choose to take a bus these are air-conditioned clean and efficient alternatively you can take a taxi but make sure to have your destination written down in Chinese and avoid traveling during doing rush hour or thunderstorms so now let's look at the typical prices Taipei is a great place to visit but of course you do need to budget your trip hostel dorm beds cost around 600 Taiwan dollars whilst private rooms in Budget Hotels begin at 1 500. five-star hotel rooms begin at around 3 000 what's recognizable Brands will cost you around five thousand food from local stores will cost you around forty dollars whilst a sit-down meal will begin at 400. Metro rides start at 30 and taxis begin at 70. so Taiwan is a fascinating place to visit and it is possible to visit Taipei on a budget when traveling around Taiwan skip the high-speed trains they are really convenient but more expensive than the local trains Budget Hotels can be found all over Taipei so use these to save money on accommodation but also make use of the local food markets to eat great food cheaply Western food is much more expensive than the local food in Taipei so skip this as often as possible take a free walking tour to see the city or visit the free temples shrines and museums available in the city as well so now let's look at the best things to do in Taipei Taipei has plenty of unique and interesting attractions to visit the National Palace Museum is home to over 70 000 artifacts from Imperial China whilst the national Taiwan museum is the oldest Museum in the country make sure you spend some time visiting the many temples in the city with the longshan temple and Confucius temple being the most popular head to the Bay 2 Hot Springs a popular destination just 30 minutes outside of downtown Taipei here you will find Resorts spas and Inns that offer a relaxing experience for reasonable prices Foodies can of course try all of the local food at the markets but also try taking a cooking class in the city too for fresh air and an escape from The Busy city of Taipei take a day trip to the nearby parks and enjoy one of the many hiking trails you could also take a day trip out to Joe fun this is a tiny historic gold mining town with preserved old streets and traditional tea houses watch this video to find out the best places to visit in Asia then for tips for backpacking Asia watch this video have a great time in Taipei and stay creative

recommendations: We share the best time to visit Taipei and when to go to Taipei, how to get to Taipei and then how to get around Taipei
We also share the best things to do in Taipei so you know why visit Taipei and know how to plan a trip to Taipei; all in this Taipei vacation guide
We recommend this drone  -
We recommend this camera -
00:23 BEST TIME TO VISIT TAIPEI
03:13 BEST THINGS TO DO IN TAIPEI

videos.title: Essential Taipei Travel Tips For Beginners - Your Ultimate Guide To Taiwan 2025
videos.description:
Planning to Travel to Taipei Taiwan in 2025
 for the first time? This video is for you!

I have been to Taipei Taiwan over a dozen times and in this video contains tips (updated to 2025
) that you NEED to know before heading there including the best time to visit, what to pack, how to get from Taoyuan Airport to Taipei City, how to use their public transportation and what to do in Taipei Taiwan!

There are CHAPTERS so feel free to skip around to the topics that are more interest, you can always watch the other parts later!

00:00 Introduction
01:48 Best Time To Visit Taipei Taiwan
02:50 Cheapest Time to Visit Taipei Taiwan

07:57 Powerbanks
09:42 Apps You Need For Taipei Taiwan - Navigation and Language Barriers
11:22 Bring Cash (Taiwanese Dollars)

12:30 How To Get From Taoyuan to Taipei

16:01 Train Etiquette

Hi I am Steve, a jaded professional living in Sydney, Australia who travels a lot for work and decided to start creating travel content on YouTube as a passion project 

I have been on Tiktok (https://www.tiktok.com/@phatlifeproject) and Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/phatlifeproject/ for some time and have a lot of short form content on there if you want to check it out or DM me for any questions

videos.transcript: Welcome to your ultimate Taipei travel guide if you've clicked on this video there's a very good chance that you're planning a trip to Taipei Taiwan and if that is the case congratulations you've made a fantastic choice! It is truly one of my favorite cities to go to as it uniquely Blends culture, technology, history, amazing food and some of the friendliest people you will ever meet! It is also in my opinion severely underrated in comparison to other Asia cities like Japan and Korea That being said heading there for the first time can be daunting for several reasons including language barriers I have actually traveled to Taipei well over a dozen times and in this video I am going to cover some of the things that you need to know before and during your travels to this wonderful City I'm going to be covering topics such as what you need to bring or prepare, when is the best time to go, how to navigate their public transportation system and most importantly how to overcome the language barriers that you might face when you're traveling to Taipei Taiwan My goal is for you to be able to travel through Taipei like a seasoned traveler by the end of this video, so let's go! hey guys it's editing Steve jumping in real quick I'm just tidying up the video now and realizing that it is a bit longer than my usual videos as I really wanted this to be quite a comprehensive guide to anybody who's traveling to Taipei Taiwan not to worry though there are chapters so do feel free to skip ahead to the chapters that you feel are more relevant to you at this particular point in time as you can always come back to the other chapters later I really want you to get as much value as you can out of this video all right with that out of the way let's jump back into the video let's start with the best time to go to Taipei Taiwan Taiwan goes through the Four Seasons Winter from December to March spring from April to June Summer from July to September member and Autumn from October to December Unsurprisingly the seasons in between winter and summer are typically the most popular times to go to taipe AKA spring and Autumn I mean apart from the optimal temperatures spring also allows you to see the cherry blossoms in bloom whereas Autumn allows you to see the entire city bathed in that trademark yellow and orange foliage with that all being said Taipei is a city that experiences a very consistent flow of external visitors throughout the year and it's generally less crowded than more popular destinations like Japan and Korea the only time that you may possibly want to avoid is between June to August AKA typhoon season but even then the city is well equipped to deal with the typhoons and you will still find plenty of things to do with that all being said if you're looking at it from a budget perspective you may want to avoid the spring and Autumn Seasons as the flight and accommodation prices may be higher than the average during these periods personally I enjoy going in Winter which is something that I have done many times before the winter temperatures in Taipei are not as Extreme as say Japan or Korea the lowest temperature that Taipei will go to in Winter would be around 13° whereas when I was in Seoul Korea last year I was walking in the middle of negative 20, NEGATIVE 20° C visiting outside of the spring and Autumn season seasons will also mean lower flight and accommodation fares with that all being said though if you're budget conscious you should also look at low cost carriers such as Air Asia and Jetstar which offer reasonably priced flight tickets almost all year round I would in fact recommend signing up for their newsletters so that you can be up to date with their latest promotion offers! Okay so you've now sorted out your flight and accommodation let's now talk about the things you need to pack prepare or even fill in before your trip to Taipei and I would strongly recommend for the first thing that you throw into your luggage bag to be a comfortable pair of walking shoes because believe you me you are going to be walking like I was averaging 20 to 30,000 steps a day easy traversing through the city most of the time locating your platform within a train station in itself is a little walking Adventure If you're going there in summer I definitely recommend bringing one of these little mobile fans and as the temperatures of Taipei Summer can go all the way up to its late 30s so definitely bring one of these and thank me later aside from that what to pack a tire wise is fairly straightforward for all other Seasons apart from Winter and if you're going there in Winter I would recommend bringing a good solid waterproof jacket my go-to brand and this is totally not sponsored is Uniqlo I mean the Japanese just do just do it better mean I don't know how they do it this jacket is quite solid it's waterproof but it still manages to be quite light you will easily survive Taipei winter in this jacket and if you're traveling you're most probably going to be needing extra luggage space either because of shopping or other reasons this is why I've always got one of these in my bag it is a fold out bag all I need to do is to open up the zip and voila it becomes a an extra bag like this pretty cool huh? By and far the most essential thing that you're going to need for your travels is your phone your smartphone is going to be your best travel buddy throughout your travels in Taipei but to use it you're going to need mobile data connection so let's talk about that There are a few options you could of course just activate your international roaming but make sure to check with your Telco provider before doing so as this could possibly end up being quite an expensive option You could also choose to pick up one of these portable Wi-Fi devices they look a little bit like this they are often on unlimited data plans and one of the advantages of this is that it allows you to connect up to 10 devices making it a fantastic option if you're traveling as a family or with a large group of friends if you plan on checking out this option I can actually get you 10% off your purchase simply go on klook.com search for wireless Wi-Fi device Taipei and enter the code PHATLIFEPROJECT when making your purchase My personal go-to though and you would already know this if you are a subscriber of mine is the Esim route for those who don't know eim is essentially a virtual SIM card that allows your phone to take on an additional Network at the same time as your current Network so that you do not have to purchase an additional physical Sim or incur expensive roaming charges another advantage of the esim option is that it will still allow your current phone number to receive sms-es and calls just make sure though that you do turn off that mobile data roaming for your local phone number you can also still use your phone as a hotspot for other devices as well most phones come with an esim slot nowadays to check if your phone has an esim slot you would simply need to look up the Eid which you will commonly find in the settings it looks a little bit like this I travel quite often and I'm actually an avid user of Airalo that gives gives you access to esim options in over 200 countries it is very easy to use simply click into the app search for your country of destination and you'll be shown all the data plan options that are available as a regular Airalo user I can actually get you $3 off your first purchase simply enter code Steve5974 I've also placed it here and in my description box when making your first purchase and thank me later but what's the use of internet connectivity if your phone is dead? This is why I also recommend you bringing a power bank or two when you're traveling I personally am a fan of this 4 in-one charger that gives me at least two full charges of my phone which is super important when I'm on the go here's the charger you can plug it straight into a Power Point to charge which is fairly convenient in itself but as the name suggests it is also a Four in one charger so while your thing is charging you can also slap on your phone like so to charge as the name suggests it is a four in one charger and so while this thing is charging you can also charge your phone it's got a Mag Safe Port right here just slap it on and way it goes can also charge other devices like for example my headphones plug it straight in like so and this is now charging as well and it can even double as your laptop adapter if you're still wanting to use your phone while you're on the go though I quite like these MagSafe charger, you slap it on like this and it will charge your phone while you're using it any power bank will do but if you are interested to purchase the ones that I'm showing here I actually got these from Snap Wireless I've been using them for well over a year and can confidently recommend them to you I can give you 10% of these devices as well simply go to the link here and also in my description box when purchasing or enter code Steve when purchasing on Snap wireless.com Speaking of your phone here are some apps that you probably want to make sure are on your phone before you head to Taipei starting with Wi-Fi map that shows you all the paid and free Wi-Fi hotspots throughout Taipei City which is a fantastic option if you're budget conscious and are looking to reduce your mobile data usage or perhaps not even purchase any form of mobile data do note though if it's the latter option your connection is going to be intermittent Google Maps is an absolute must when you're traveling in Taipei Taiwan you already know how to use the app so I'm not going to subject you through that but this particular app is quite helpful when asking for directions or when riding a cab if you are facing language barriers in communicating where you want to go simply enter the location that you want to head to in Google map, scroll down and show the person that you're trying to communicate with the Chinese address or even level up this hack by copying the address and pasting it into Google translate and press DICTATE In fact one of the first Chinese addresses that I recommend you have handy on your phone is your hotel or accommodation address keep this with you at all times so you can easily get back to your accommodation or hotel if you ever get lost Speaking of Google translate a lot of the menus at restaurants and cafes will be in Chinese especially when you venture away from the city center or if you head to the famous restaurants that locals go to but if you're faced with that situation and not to worry simply open up your Google Translate app turn on the camera and place your phone over the menu like so and voila you are now ready to order! Whilst Taipei is an advanced City, hotels restaurants and major shopping malls will take credit cards I would still recommend you have some cash on hand especially if you plan on checking out the Taiwanese Night Market scene which by the way I highly recommend that you do if you don't have any cash on hand not to worry there are plenty of money exchange counters once you exit Customs at the Taoyuan Airport arrivals speaking of Customs let's now talk entry requirements this will obviously depend on your country of origin but in most cases you do not require a visa to enter Taipei Taiwan provided that you're only there for a maximum of 90 days for business or Leisure, have at least 6 months validity on your passport and have a return or ongoing flat ticket out of Taiwan If you're not sure on your visa requirements it is always good to double check with the Taiwan immigration website a link here and also in my description box if you do fall in the Visa free category all you need to do is to fill in an online entry form prior to entry again I've placed the link here and also in my description box All righty we're now in Taipei, welcome to Taipei! let's now talk about how we can get you from the Taoyuan airport to Taipei where your accommodation is as there are several options Starting with the fastest way which is via the Taoyuan airport MRT Express line which goes from Taoyuan airport to Taipei main station in just under 38 minutes there's one every 15 minutes and there's even wireless charging and free Wi-Fi on board tickets are also cheap at 160 Taiwanese dollars or around $5 US or around 7.50 AUD all you need to do to find the train is to follow the train signs once you've exited the international arrivals signs pointing towards the train station is available in both English and Chinese so don't worry about getting lost just be aware also that if your accommodation is not close to the Taipei main station that you'll have to do a transfer at Taipei Main Station to the train station that is closest to your hotel or accommodation of choice and Google Maps will help you determine the name of the station you need to get to once you get to Taipei main station do note that the MRT only operates between 6:00 a.m. to 11:00 p.m. and so if your arrival or departure time is outside of that time window you may need to consider other forms of transportation such as buses there are three buses to choose from the 1819, 1960 and 1961 and their services run till around midnight with tickets costing around $140 Taiwanese dollars now the bus option does take longer at around an hour but it could work out to be a better option for you depending on where your accommodation is you see the 1819 will take you to the Taipei main station much like the express MRT option but the 1960 will take you to Taipei 101 or the Taipei city hall area The buses also only come every 30 minutes so you may want to also factor that into your decision these options are in my opinion better suited to its solo Travelers who are traveling light if you are traveling with family kids elderly folk and lots of luggage bags their birth options may not be feasible for you yes there are services that you can Avail to that deliver your luggage from the airport to the hotel I did have a check and it cost around $20 a bag which is why I would recommend that you catch a cab instead Taoyuan is about a 50 km distance away from Taipei City and a cab costs to take you from the airport into the city is generally around 1,200 to 1,500 Taiwanese dollars AKA around $37 to $46 US or 56 to 70 Aussie dollars depending on where your accommodation is which I think is a pretty reasonable amount if you're traveling again with a large group or with plenty of luggages okay you're all checked in and ready to explore Taipei the first thing I recommend you do is head to a metro station or your closest convenience store to get yourselves one of this this is an easy card AKA a yo-yo ka it will cost you 100 Taiwanese dollars to get and it has zero balance on it when you get it but you can top this card up at basically any convenience store and at any Metro station via machines that look like this with the card all topped up all you need to do is tap and enter the train station to ride the trains but that's not all this card does it is also a cash card that you can use to make payments at any place that has this sign listed as a payment method and that includes cabs and yeah now you have one of these and you're ready to explore the city using the trains let's now talk train etiquette trains known as jieyun and Taipei trains are immaculately maintained and as such food drinks and especially chewing gum are not allowed there are also organized lines for you to aboard the train make sure you only stand at those lines to avoid getting in the way of the passengers who are getting off please also avoid loud conversations or taking calls when you're on the train and hot tip these blue seats are specially reserved for those in need including the elderly, pregnant and people with disabilities so please refrain from sitting on these unless you fall into these categories and whilst not as bad as Japan peak hour traveling in Taipei will also mean fairly full carriages so if you can please refrain from traveling during peak hours all major cities in Taiwan including Taipei, Kaohsiung and Taichung have excellent water quality standards their tap water quality meets or exceeds International regulatory standards for both taste and safety and that means that yes the tap water in taipe is drinkable another thing that you may want to know before heading to Taipei is that earthquakes do happen every now and then not to worry though as the city's infrastructure is built to be earthquake proof just follow the protocol of Duck and Cover if you do happen to be there when an earthquake happens looking for things to do in Taipei? I'm glad you asked for there are so many I have previously done a video highlighting 14 free or cheap things that you can do in Taipei I will link that video at the end of this video so that you can go ahead and watch that next if you like food night markets which by the way is a quintessential thing you must do when you're in Taipei there are three main night markets in Taipei you must check out Shilin, Ningxia and Raohe I have created a list of cheap, must try Michelin rated food spots at each of these night markets and you will find these videos in my Taipei playlist which I will place again at the end of this video by the way I'm Steve from the phatlife project my channel uploads food and travel content on a weekly basis if that is your Vibe I strongly recommend subscribing I do hope that you found this video helpful informative or perhaps just a little bit entertaining if it has don't forget to like comment and share it really does help appease the YouTube algorithm Gods but more importantly it just makes my day as a content creator and if you got to this point of the video I just want to thank you so much for your support it really does mean everything I do hope that you have a fantastic day ahead or that you've already had a good day I will see you very soon in the next video, ciao!

videos.transcript: welcome to Taipei the capital of Taiwan in this video I'll share my experience on where to stay in Taipei as a first-time visitor I recommend two areas to stay first is seam and ding second is around taipe main station seamen ding is one of taipei's most iconic neighborhoods it's a hub for youth culture shopping food and entertainment known for its vibrant streets and Youthful energy the area is popular hangout spot for young people and tourists who flock to its streets to socialize sh dine and express themselves staying and seening allows you to immerse yourself in the energy and excitement of one of Tai's most dynamic neighborhoods okay see seening boast a wide range of eateries ranging from food stalls restaurants to cafes and bubble tea shops the district is a concentrated shopping mecca featuring a diverse array of retail stores boutique style shops shopping malls and Street vendors at night seening is known for its Lively and bustling atmosphere with Lively pedestrian streets colorful lights and a constant Buzz of activity it's a pedestrian area and a walking District meaning it's easy to explore by foot and bicycle you'll be close to taipe main station and the national Taiwan University Hospital less than a 10-minute bicycle ride for each seening is a great choice for Budget Travelers Backpackers and those looking for a fun and energetic emisphere Taipei main station is a major Transportation Hub staying near here will allow you to explore other parts of Taipei and beond with ease making it a convenient base for your travels this convergence focal point connects various modes of transportation including the taipe Metro MRT Taiwan highspeed rail HSR and bus services you'll be able to connect directly to the airport and visit other cities in Taiwan such as GA taipe main station serves as a meeting point for tours such as visiting shifen and jufen taipe Main Station operates 24 hours a day whether you need to catch an early morning coffee or grab a late night snack taipe main station has you covered with its 24-hour Services various shopping malls are in the vicinity there are food courts and abundant restaurant options you'll also be in proximity to ninga nightmar Market DEA Street cending and National Taiwan University Hospital between a 5 to 10minute bicycle ride for each location I highly recommend using the ubikes to explore Taipei there are many stations to take out and return the bicycles it costs 10 tai1 or 32 C USD for half an hour Fu Hong beef noodles serves delicious Taiwanese beef noodle soup 120 Taiwan dollars for a large bowl open 24 hours a Vietnamese restaurant and C mending on cming Street Run by an impressive Vietnamese lady who is the chef server and cashier at the same time the food is authentic and delicious $140 Taiwan dollars for beefa or $4.50 USD dly beef noodle restaurant the broth is fragrant here and has a lighter but flavorful note to it the meat is good quality and the soft tenant pieces melts in your mouth $230 towan for their signature bowl or $740 USD leang pin beef noodles another delicious place in sding to get Taiwanese beef noodle soup $180 Taiwan dollar for a bowl or $580 USD Lu Shandong is a popular place with locals for Taiwanese beef noodle soup if you're craving Ramen or sikim there's a small shop in seening on changu road I got a shrimp miso Sim in here for 300 Taiwan dollars that had shrimp dumplings pork slices green onions a sweet egg steamed cabbage over thick round noodles with fresh chopped onions and garlic on the side Louisa coffee is a nice place to relax with an IC tii tea coffee 100 Taiwan dollar for a cup or $320 cents USD popular with locals it's open until 10: p.m. at night with three air conditioned floors and is a cheaper alternative to Starbucks across the street I enjoyed getting a fresh fruit Boba milk tea from this shop from Cen ding my first stop was to bka L xang Temple a Buddhist temple honoring Guang Yin the goddess of Mercy I did the next six activities All in One Day by using a combination of taipe Metro Transit and U [Music] bikes covering three night markets two temples and one cultural Landmark our second stop is to Shen Kai Sheek Memorial Hall which is also home to the National Concert Hall the National Theater Hall Liberty Square Arch and Liberty Square Plaza you'll be able to witness four iconic buildings and immerse yourself in a grand and beautiful space our third stop is to Shong Shang suu Temple and R Night Market located right next to each other and right in front of s Shang station once you step in the air is filled with aromatic incense you're surrounded by ornate decorations colorful lanterns and liveliness of locals praying the temple is dedicated to Matsu the goddess of the sea and protector of sailors fishermen and travelers worshippers come to pray for Prosperity blessings and safety during travels casual visitors come for a cultural experience and spiritual reflection I recommend going at Sunset to nighttime when the temple lights and lanterns are lit the mural behind the temple is especially beautiful right outside the temple is the entrance to R Night Market R Night Market takes about 30 minutes to explore I recommend trying the pork buttons at the entrance our fourth stop is shayin Night Market we explored here for around 40 minutes minutes I recommend trying the peanut ice cream crap there's also a popular stall that sells grilled King oyster mushrooms supposedly really delicious our fifth stop is ninga night market our visit here took about 25 minutes to explore this one is shorter and smaller compared to the previous two night markets making it easier to navigate we really like the egg sausage wrap from ninga Night Market we headed back to seing ding if you need a place to exercise taipe Wong Sports Center is located in seinging the center has an outside soccer court at badmington court a workout gym a pingpong and virtual golf floor and a swimming pool I paid $110 Taiwan dollars to go for a swim or $351 USD I joined a bus tour for $25 USD to visit shein and jufen outside of taipe surrounding schin old Street are Lush creen mountains and and Scenic Countryside Landscapes chin oat Street features a Historic Railway track running through the center of the street home to numerous food stalls fried chicken wings and soup dumplings shaen oat street is famous for its sky lanterns used for sending wishes and prayers into the sky you can purchase sky lanterns from vendors along the street for $250 Taiwan dollars or $8 USD write your wishes messages and drawings on them and release them into the sky for good luck and prosperity as the vendor ERS capture the moment for you second stop of the tour was to Sheen waterfall a famous waterfall in Taiwan You observe from viewing platforms and observation decks but it's a waterfall you cannot go in as you walk along a paved path back to the exit the area is surrounded by lush green forest has a playground for kids and seating and tables for eating from food vendors [Music] there last stop jufen old Street a historic town nestled in the mountains lined with narrow Alleyways Stone staircases wooden buildings adorned with Red Lanterns traveling back in time to Old World Taiwan a te house an iconic and historic tea house located in juin perched on the hillside overlooking the town and Coastline you can relax on the outdoor Terrace and enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and sea while sipping on a cup of tea from here you can admire the sunset dipping below the mountain peaks while overlooking the expanse of the ocean $300 Taiwan dollars for a small pot of tea and a few pastries or $9.60 USD juin is best to visit starting at 600 p.m. when the Sun starts to set and the lanterns start to light up it's also cooler in the evening and at night making it more pleasant to walk around and explore I would primarily go for the vibe and the atmosphere of the lit lanterns and the beautiful view of the sea and mountains at Sunset there's a diverse array of street food stalls and eateries offering Taiwanese snacks Delicacies and drinks the scenery here is akin to stepping into a scene straight out of Spirited Away the animated film by Studio [Music] jii [Music] if you're going to or leaving from Taiwan you most likely have a layover in Tokyo haneda Airport I'll share some scenes during my stop there Hana airport offers a wide range of facilities including lounges an observation deck baggage storage dutyfree shops restaurants cafes and free Wi-Fi throughout its terminals Hana airport is known for its efficiency conven I and punctuality and has consistently ranked amongst the world's best airports in terms of Passenger satisfaction offering Travelers a seamless and pleasant experience from checkin to [Music] Bard if you're visiting taipe soon the international airport is quite far from the city I have a quick video video guide on how to get from the airport to Taipei you can find the link at the end of this video or in the video description below thank you for joining me this week in taipe Taiwan I hope to runu with you at our next destination
